Dear Mr. Dorian,
MECO Engineering Company, Inc. (MECO) is pleased to present qualifications to the City of
Hannibal for professional engineering services to assist the City in drafting specifications to
demolish the “Former St. Elizabeth Hospital”. 
OUR FIRM
MECO Engineering is celebrating its 40th anniversary this year. This long-spanning career has
been built on serving municipalities, such as yours, in a vast span of projects with varying
needs, requirements, funding, and challenges. We take pride in gaining the majority of our
business from repeat, satisfied customers. 
MECO currently has six office locations, with a location conveniently close in Hannibal, MO,
mere miles from the former hospital. This, as well as our knowledge of the area and ever-
changing regulations, and our extensive experience specifically in structural evaluations, allows
us to assure you of our ability to serve the City in a high-quality, cost-effective manner. 
YOUR PROJECT
As a member of the Hannibal community for 40 years, MECO understands the piece of history
the former St. Elizabeth Hospital holds in Hannibal. Our engineering firm, MECO A/E, was slated
to do renovation/rehabilitation work for the former hospital at one point, but due to challenges,
including hazardous materials, the project fell through, as so many have. We are deeply familiar
with the area, the building, and the regulations necessary to demolish this building in a safe
manner, and one that gives the respect to the surrounding neighborhood and homes of which
they are deserving. We will be glad to sit down with the City and talk through best options with
special consideration for safety, and possible preservations of pieces of this long-standing
Hannibal pillar.

YOUR TEAM
MECO currently has 60 professionals with many talents and continues to welcome more team
members to our dedicated team as we expand. Our firm is well-positioned and highly qualified
to be considered as your consultant and engineer for your selection process based on
expertise, knowledge, and experience. 
Your specialized team for your project will include:
James D. Bensman, PE, SE, Project Manager, Structural Engineer
Matt Cissi, EI, Structural Engineer
Robert Hogan, PMP, Hannibal Manager
Kevin Garnett, PE, Civil Engineer
This highly qualified team, and the years of projects they bring with them, allow us to
guarantee our abilities to handle any and all needs associated with structural assessment and
evaluation, the safe handling of hazardous materials, environmental and regulatory
compliance, preparation of demolition plans, including bidding and construction administration
services, and permitting assistance to make this process as painless as possible for the City. 
OUR APPROACH
MECO’s “Standard of Care” and “Project Approach” is a team-centric based philosophy to
ensure that every step of project scope development and design review fully invests the client
in each step of the process. MECO does not believe in an engineer-driven project
development process; we see the most value in investing our client input in the process, first
and foremost. Our clients will always lead the initiative for developing projects and working
with our professional staff to determine the best suited solutions to their needs. 
You can rely on MECO and our Project Team to provide the attention to detail, prompt
response, and a level of commitment to service, experience, knowledge, and expertise that
you require and expect of your Engineering Consultant. We never forget that clients have
many choices and options in their selection process for a consulting engineering firm. We
would be honored to be selected to serve the District in this capacity.
For questions, or to meet with your project team, please feel free to contact through phone or
email at 573-221-4048 or jbensman@mecoengineering.com.
Sincerely yours, 
MECO Engineering Company, Inc. 
James D. Bensman, PE, SE
Senior Principal Engineer | Project Manager

MECO provided structural engineering services to complete a demolition-related
structural investigation of two buildings located at 744 Mortimer Street, Barry, Il.
Services included performing structural observations in the areas of the buildings
to be demolished and possible affected by demolition, preparation of a letter-report
with demolition recommendations, followed up by contract documents, structural
observations and data collection, and construction phase engineering services. 

Provided engineering services for modifications to existing buildings at Harbison-Walker
Refractories Company plant at 1301 Westminster Ave, Fulton, MO. Services provided
included structural analyses and design of bracing for two (2) steel-framed and metal
sheeted buildings that remain after removal of part of the 1947 plant building lcoated in the
center part of the building complex. One separation/cut was proposed at the northerly end
of the building complex to isolate Building #1B, and a second separation/cut was proposed
at the southerly end to isolate either framelines 1 through 4 or 1 through 8. Drawings
showing proposed bracing and other required structural details were generated for the
owner's use in completing the modifications. 
Additional Services were requested which included structural modifications to existing
Tunnel Kiln building at Harbison-Walker Refractories Company plant at 1301 Westminster
Ave, Fulton, MO. Basic Services provided included onsite data collection and observation of
existing conditions, structural analyses and design of new wall system along south end of
proposed north building "cut" at Column Line 42. Also performed design of new wall and roof
panels to be installed on the separated south and north buildings, that were to remain after
proposed removal of the middle part of the 1947 Tunnel Kiln plant building. Drawings
showing proposed structural details and specifications will be generated for the owner's
use in constructing the modifications.

There are many ways to measure past performance and the standards are different for each. However,
we firmly hold that our actions that result in client satisfaction and ultimately, client retention, are the
best measure of success. Our goal is to provide a quality project, on time and on budget.
Control of Costs
MECO estimates the project costs based on historical bids in cooperation with the current industry
rates. Once the project has been awarded, we track each of the contracts under construction and the
allocated amount awarded. Monthly pay applications submitted are reviewed and approved. Should
change orders be necessary, they are incorporated within the excel sheet of each contract where
funds are taken from the contingencies to increase their specific area contracts. We follow the project
from conceptual project drawings and estimates through preliminary engineering reports, financing
document submittals, the bidding process, and final construction costs. Final lean waivers for material
suppliers and affidavits for prevailing wages are obtained to ensure all is in order prior to final payment
and completion of projects. This process allows MECO to keep a close eye on cost and keep the
county fully informed of the progress of the project. 
Quality Control/ Quality Assurance
Our Quality Control/Quality Assurance Team is comprised of principals/senior engineers who bring
their years of combined experience and knowledge to the review table. 
Team Members:
Kevin Garnett, PE, Executive Vice President
Donald Jenkins, PE, Executive Vice President
Ryan Huseman, PE, Senior Engineer 
Scott E. Vogler, PE, Senior Principal Engineer
Max F. Middendorf, PE, Senior Principal Engineer
James D. Bensman, PE, SE, Senior Principal Engineer
The leader of the QC/QA Team is determined by the engineering discipline and project scope of
services. For this project, the leader will be Max F. Middendorf. Project concept, design plans and
drawings, technical specifications, contract documents, and estimates of probable cost, project
schedule and budget will be closely reviewed by Mr. Middendorf during the design phase.
Construction phase will bring review of project progress to determine compliance with construction
schedule/deadlines, review of change orders, quantities, pay requests, inspector’s logs, and
compliance with project budget and schedule. The majority of our business is repeat business. This is
the direct result of satisfied customers.

P i t t s f i e l d ,  I L
The City of Pittsfield, IL undertook a major water system improvements project in 2009 to
provide a safe and reliable drinking water supply to their community. 
Supply
In order to meet state and federal standards for drinking water, the city realized the need to
replace their existing surface water supply (lake), with a ground water supply provided
through a new alluvial well field, including two 1,000 GPM wells (approximately 95-ft),
submersible pumps, elevated platforms, piping and electrical. 
Treatment
The new ground water supply is treated by a new 1,000 GPM Ion-Exchange ground water
softening plant. A new SCADA system links all systems and facilities to monitor and control
the operations. A diesel-powered emergency backup generator provides emergency service
to the two new wells and a diesel-powered generator has capacity to operate the new water
treatment plant at full capacity.

Water System Improvements: $5.6M water system expansion (two 750,000 gallon elevated
towers/ one new 300 GPM well/pumping 2 stations/59,700 LF mains); project coordination,
planning, consulting. Project also consisted of: 57,000 LF new waterline (owner-

and maintenance building.
MECO became the Water District’s Engineer in 2002. Since then, Wells #1 and #2 were
chemically treated, motor and controls were elevated and the pumps/motors were
replaced. Well #5 was also completed at this time with a submersible turbine pump installed
to deliver an additional 500 GPM. Well #6 was completed in 2012 to provide an additional
300 GPM to the district for treatment.
In 2019 MECO was also hired for a Water Treatment Plant expansion project to add 3 new
dual stage filters and 1,000 gpm clarifier, and new chemical feed systems, a new motor
control center and SCADA controls.

The scope of services was for the design engineering of the basement area for Louisiana’s
Water Treatment Plant to address corrective piping to drains, dripping seals and leaking
fittings. To reduce the humidity from the basement, a design to seal off this area will be
incorporated with the installation of a HVAC/Remote air handler. The awarded contractor
will coordinate the rental of a commercial dehumidifying unit to reduce the moisture and
will be paid by the city. Once the moisture has been reduced, then the basement can
continue to be addressed with the identified replacement of corroded fasteners and bolts
as well as prepping and painting of existing piping. The plans will also include a separate
sheet to address the installation of a new door and frame at the pump house as identified /
requested by the City of Louisiana.
Construction engineering covered the pre-construction meeting as well as progress
meetings, the administration paperwork associated with contract documentation and
required bonds/insurance as well as any pay application, change orders, and final
paperwork. Construction engineering also covered the part-time inspection portion to
cover the critical time an inspector needs to be present or follow up on ensuring the work
has been completed with inspection reports and photographs.

MECO Engineering Company

There are many ways to measure past performance and the standards are different for each. However,
we firmly hold that our actions that result in client satisfaction and ultimately, client retention, are the
best measure of success. Our goal is to provide a quality project, on time and on budget.
Control of Costs
MECO estimates the project costs based on historical bids in cooperation with the current industry
rates. Once the project has been awarded, we track each of the contracts under construction and the
allocated amount awarded. Monthly pay applications submitted are reviewed and approved. Should
change orders be necessary, they are incorporated within the excel sheet of each contract where
funds are taken from the contingencies to increase their specific area contracts. We follow the project
from conceptual project drawings and estimates through preliminary engineering reports, financing
document submittals, the bidding process, and final construction costs. Final lean waivers for material
suppliers and affidavits for prevailing wages are obtained to ensure all is in order prior to final payment
and completion of projects. This process allows MECO to keep a close eye on cost and keep the
county fully informed of the progress of the project.

Our Quality Control/Quality Assurance Team is comprised of principals/senior engineers who bring
their years of combined experience and knowledge to the review table.

MECO is directed by the University of Missouri - Columbia, Campus Facilities
Planning and Design Construction Division each year to study, evaluate, and
recommend solutions for parking lot areas located throughout the campus and in
various stages of need for maintenance repair and/or reconstruction. Pavements
are evaluated and various options for repair or rebuild are considered. Opinions of
probable project costs are prepared, reviewed by the University, and the option
that both upgrades the existing lot and fits within the budget constraints is
determined for each of the targeted parking lots. 
Options considered for each included seal coats, chip and seal surfacing, milling of
existing pavements and placing new asphalt overlays and/or chip and seal
surfacing, as well as concrete pavements replaced. The extent of the existing state
of deterioration controlled the extent of repair or reconstruction. Plans and
specifications are prepared and provided to the University for the university's use in
the bidding of the projects, provided project administration (awarding, change
orders, etc.) and generally we conduct construction observation services for the
project.
